content:
# High Frequency Sequences

**Summary**: Short-term cycles of depositional and/or diagenetic events.

---

## How It Appears in the Thesis Collection

- [[2014-sami|Sami, 2014]] identified high-frequency-sequences to map the stratigraphic architecture of the Upper Khartam Member.

## Scientific Context

- [[2014-sami|Sami, 2014]] HFSs provide a high-resolution view of the carbonate ramp's evolution.

## Measurement and Characterization Methods

- [[2014-sami|Sami, 2014]] Stratigraphic mapping and interpretation.

## Key Findings Across Sources

- The study identified three distinct HFSs (HFS-1, 2, and 3) within the sampled outcrop section. ([[2014-sami|Sami, 2014, p. 103]])

## Contrasting Values and Interpretations

- [[2014-sami|Sami, 2014]] HFS-1: 6.5m, HFS-2: 6m, HFS-3: 5m.

## Open Questions and Research Gaps
